name: Suspicious Coworker Behavior Report
description: Internal form for employees to confidentially report concerning behavior by another employee — accessing systems outside their role, exfiltrating data to USB drives, photographing screens, working unusual hours on sensitive systems, etc. Submissions are reviewed by a small, trained security group only. Reporter identity is protected and never shared with the reported person's manager. Submitting a report does not get anyone fired — it triggers an investigation that follows company policy.
title: Report a Workplace Security Concern
intro:
If you've noticed something about a coworker that doesn't feel right — accessing systems they shouldn't, taking data out of the office, photographing screens, working odd hours on sensitive systems — this is the place to tell us. A few things to know before you start:

- **You can submit this anonymously.** The email field is optional. If you leave it blank, we have no way to identify you.
- **Your report is not sent to that person's manager.** It goes to a small, trained security group who reviews it discreetly.
- **You are not getting anyone fired by submitting this.** We investigate carefully, follow company policy, and most reports turn out to be explainable. Reporting is the right thing to do — even if it turns out to be nothing.
- **You don't need proof.** A pattern you've noticed is enough. We'll take it from there.

Thank you for trusting us with this.
submit_message: Thank you. We take every report seriously and review it with care. If you provided an email, you'll receive a case reference shortly. Otherwise, this report has been received and will be reviewed by our security team.

fields.label: Your email (optional)
fields.help: Leave this blank to submit anonymously. If you provide it, we can follow up with questions and give you a case reference. Your identity is never shared with the person you're reporting or their manager.

fields.label: What have you noticed?
fields.help: Describe what you've seen in your own words. Specific examples help, but a general pattern is fine too. You don't need to be certain — just honest about what you observed.
fields.sample_value: Have noticed John in Finance accessing customer payment dashboards multiple times after hours over the past three weeks, even though his role doesn't require it. Last Tuesday around 9pm I walked past his desk and saw him photographing a spreadsheet with his personal phone. He closed the screen quickly when he saw me.

fields.label: Which of these apply?
fields.help: Pick anything that fits what you've observed. Skip if you're not sure.
options: Accessing systems or data not related to their role
Taking data out of the office (USB drives, personal email, cloud storage)
Photographing or recording screens with a personal device
Working unusual hours on sensitive systems
Asking coworkers for credentials or access they shouldn't need
Sudden change in attitude or behavior (resentful, secretive, stressed)
Talking about leaving the company or a competitor
Bringing in unauthorized devices or installing unapproved software
Bypassing security controls (propping doors, sharing badges)
Other concerning pattern
sample_value: Accessing systems or data not related to their role
Photographing or recording screens with a personal device
Working unusual hours on sensitive systems

fields.label: How long has this been going on?
options: I just noticed today
A few days
A couple of weeks
About a month
Several months
Longer than six months
Not sure

fields.label: What team or role is this person in?
fields.help: You don't have to name them here — a role or team is enough to help us route this (e.g., 'Finance', 'IT contractor', 'Sales rep'). If you'd rather name them, you can do that in the description above.

fields.label: Has anyone else mentioned similar concerns?
fields.help: Optional. Helps us understand if this is a known pattern.
options: No, just me
One or two other people have mentioned something similar
Several people seem aware
I haven't talked to anyone about this
Not sure
fields.sample_value: I haven't talked to anyone about this

fields.label: Anything else we should know?
fields.help: Optional. Anything about the situation, your relationship with this person, or context that might help us investigate fairly.
fields.sample_value: I want to be clear — I'm not trying to get John in trouble. He's been under a lot of stress lately and I genuinely don't know if there's an innocent explanation. I just felt like someone should know.

alert_template.title: Workplace security concern submitted
